commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(docker): increase healthcheck timeout to 10s
target.body:
## Summary

Increase Docker healthcheck timeout from 5s to 10s for the openclaw-gateway service.

**Problem:**
- 5s timeout is too short for realistic container startup
- CI environments with slower I/O often see false negatives
- Healthy containers get marked unhealthy before they're ready

**Why it matters:**
- Reduces CI flakiness and unnecessary restarts
- Aligns with Docker's recommended minimum (10s) for service checks
- Improves reliability without sacrificing failure detection speed

**What changed:**
- `docker-compose.yml`: `timeout: 5s` → `timeout: 10s`

**What did NOT change:**
- No code changes, only configuration
- No behavior changes for running containers
- No impact on production deployments (configurable via env)

## Change Type
- [x] Chore/infra
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening

## Scope
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Test Plan
- [x] CI will run docker-compose checks automatically
- [x] No manual testing required for config-only change

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No (default value only)
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations
None. This is a conservative increase that only makes healthchecks more tolerant.
target.author: zehanxu-doncic
